JCI Rises 0.58% at Open as Oil Retreats and Ramadan Consumption Outlook Holds
body: JCI advances to 7,484 as oil eases from recent spikes while investors watch global conflict risks and domestic demand.

title: Finance Minister Drops Plan to Dissolve Customs Directorate After Reform Progress
body: Purbaya drops plan to dissolve the Customs Directorate after reforms and improved performance earn praise from President Prabowo.

title: Finance Minister Swears in 1,500 Officials, Issues Corruption Warning
body: Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa warns officials they are not above the law as he swears in 1,500 staff amid tax and customs reforms.

title: Indonesian Customs Targets Luxury Watch Boutiques Over Suspected Illegal Imports
body: Jakarta Customs tightens inspections of luxury watch retailers to curb undeclared imports and ensure compliance with tax and customs rules.

title: Rupiah Climbs to Rp 16,862, Analysts See More Gains Despite Shrinking FX Reserves
body: Rupiah strengthens to Rp 16,862 and may gain further Wednesday as easing Middle East tensions support sentiment despite falling FX reserves.

title: 3.5 Million Vehicles Set to Leave Jakarta for Lebaran Exodus, Peak Traffic Expected March 18
body: About 3.5 million vehicles are expected to leave Jakarta for Lebaran, with peak outbound traffic projected on March 18.

title: Jakarta Stocks Jump 1.41% as Trump Signals Iran War Nearing End, Oil Drops Below $100
body: Jakarta stocks rose 1.41% to 7,440 as Trump signaled the Iran war may soon end, sending oil below $100 and lifting global risk sentiment.

title: Indonesia’s Retail Sales Dip 2.7% in January, BI Reports
body: Indonesia’s retail sales fell 2.7% in January as post-holiday spending cooled, but Bank Indonesia expects a rebound ahead of Ramadan.

title: Mandiri Posts Rp 8.9 Trillion Profit as of February
body: Bank Mandiri booked Rp 8.9T net profit by February 2026, up 16.7% yoy, driven by surging Livin’ transactions and strong loan growth.

title: BI to Pause Key Payment Systems During Eid Holiday Week
body: Bank Indonesia will suspend several payment and monetary systems from March 18–24 for the Eid holiday. BI-FAST transfers remain available.

title: JCI Jumps 1.44% at Open as Trump Signals Iran War May Be Short-Lived, Oil Prices Retreat
body: JCI jumped 1.44% at the open after Trump hinted the Iran war may be brief, easing geopolitical fears and pushing oil prices lower.

title: Indonesia to Review Budget if Oil Prices Stay Above $100
body: Indonesia may review its state budget within a month if oil prices stay above $100, as Middle East tensions push global energy costs.

title: Indonesian Consumer Confidence Weakens
body: The February survey showed that the Indonesian consumer confidence index hit 125.2 points, down from 127 the previous month.

title: Indonesia to Accelerate Ethanol Blending as Oil Prices Surge
body: Indonesia plans to accelerate ethanol blending in gasoline as global oil prices surge above $118 per barrel.

title: Bahlil: No Subsidized Fuel Price Hike Until Eid Holiday
body: Indonesia will keep subsidized fuel prices unchanged until the Eid holiday despite global oil prices surging past $100 per barrel.

title: Jakarta Stocks Tumbled 3.27% as Oil Shock Sparks Fears for Indonesia’s State Budget
body: Indonesia’s JCI fell 3.27% to 7,337 as oil surged above $100 amid escalating Middle East tensions, triggering broad selloffs across Asia.

title: Indonesia Won’t Cut Free School Meal Budget Despite Oil Price Surge
body: Indonesia will not cut its $19.7B free meal program despite rising oil prices that could increase energy subsidy costs.

title: Oil Above $100 Sends Rupiah Past Rp17,000
body: Oil’s sharp rally above $100 per barrel pressured emerging market currencies, pushing the rupiah past Rp17,000.

title: Indonesia Downplays Oil Price Spike as Minister Says Fiscal Risks Manageable
body: Indonesia says rising oil prices could increase subsidy costs but insists fiscal stability remains intact.

title: Hormuz Tensions Threaten Indonesia Auto Industry With 2–4% Cost Surge
body: Tensions around the Strait of Hormuz could raise Indonesia’s auto production costs by up to 4% while disrupting exports and supply chains.
